title: Curry Fish
excerpt: Ingredients 2 kg stockfish cleaned and filleted fish spice oil for frying the fish 1 bottle brown vinegar 250 ml water 200 ml sugar 30 ml turmeric 80 ml curry powder 7.5 ml salt 20 ml peppercorns 60 ml pickling spice 6 onions sliced into rings 6 bay leaves 40 ml cornflour Method Cut …
html:
Ingredients

  • 2 kg stockfish cleaned and filleted
  • fish spice
  • oil for frying the fish
  • 1 bottle brown vinegar
  • 250 ml water
  • 200 ml sugar
  • 30 ml turmeric
  • 80 ml curry powder
  • 7.5 ml salt
  • 20 ml peppercorns
  • 60 ml pickling spice
  • 6 onions sliced into rings
  • 6 bay leaves
  • 40 ml cornflour

Method

Cut the fish into pieces. Pat dry with kitchen paper. Sprinkle fish spice over and fry in oil until just
cooked through – not mushy.

Drain on kitchen paper.

Put all the remaining ingredients except the fish and cornflour into the pot. Put the lid on and cook
for 10 minutes.

Take a little of the hot sauce and mix it with the cornflour in a cup.

Pour the mixture back into the sauce and let it cook until the sauce starts to thicken.

In a plastic or glass dish, pour a little of the sauce. Now layer the fish and sauce. Cover and put in the
fridge. The longer the better.

Easy!
